nameMeanings.name: Washbourne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English: locative name from Great and Little Washbourne (Gloucs) or Washbourne in Halwell (Devon). The variant Washburn could also be from Washburn (WR Yorks), but there is comparatively little evidence that this place yielded a surname.

variants.name: Washburn
meanings: English: habitational name from Great and Little Washbourne (Gloucestershire), or Washbourne in Halwell (Devon). The Gloucestershire placename derives from Old English wæsse (genitive wæssan) ‘wet place, swamp, marsh’ + burna ‘spring, stream’. The Devon placename derives from Old English wæsce ‘place for washing’ + burna.
History: John Washburn came to MA in 1626 and settled in Duxbury, MA, in 1632.

nameMeanings.name: IV
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Cambodian: written អ៊ីវ, unexplained. — Note: In the population figure published by the US Census Bureau, the Roman number IV (meaning ‘the fourth’ of the four bearers of the name) may also be counted as a surname Iv.

nameMeanings.name: John
meanings: Greek: the Lord gave graciously; whom Jehovah gave; whom Jehovah bestowed.

en.lifeSummary: When John Washbourne IV Knight was born in 1180, in Worcestershire, England, United Kingdom, his father, William d' Estham, was 25 and his mother, Joan Perole, was 16. He had at least 1 son with Lady Margaret Poher. He died in 1230, in his hometown, at the age of 50.
